description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, cylindrical in shape with a domed top and flanged base. The hydrant features three prominent hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one facing forward, each capped with a hexagonal nut. The surface is painted a bright, slightly weathered red, with visible wear and minor scuff marks, suggesting outdoor exposure. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut, used for opening the valve to release water. The body is constructed from heavy cast metal, likely iron or steel, and is designed to be highly durable and resistant to impact. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant is shown isolated without any curb or sidewalk context.
description: A robust, red, cylindrical fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets and a domed top, showing signs of outdoor wear. The surface is hard, metallic, and slightly rough.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main hose outlet and central operating nut, with both side outlets partially visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the side hose outlets and the domed top, with the front outlet visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two side hose outlets symmetrically, with the front outlet not visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other side hose outlet and the domed top, with the front outlet visible in profile.
